name: Tips for Storing & Freezing Cookies
recipeInstructions:
STORING AND FREEZING COOKIES:
1. Separate soft and crisp textured cookies for storage. Soft cookies should be kept in tightly covered containers. Crisp cookies may be stored in a cookie jar or container with loose fitting lid in a dry climate. In humid climates, store crisp cookies in a tightly covered container.
2. If soft cookies begin to dry out, add a piece of apple or bread to the container to help them retain moisture. If crisp cookies become soggy and are not decorated, heat in an oven set to 300°F (150°C) for 3-5 minutes to crisp them.
3. Store frosted cookies and thumbprint cookies in a single layer in a tightly covered container. If space is needed, layer cookies between waxed paper to protect.
4. Bar cookies may be stored, tightly covered, in baking pan. Some may require refrigeration due to ingredients used.
5. Unbaked cookie dough can be refrigerated for up to one week or frozen for up to 6 weeks. Rolls of dough should be sealed tightly in plastic wrap; other doughs should be stored in airtight containers.
6. Crisp cookies freeze better than soft cookies. Bars and brownies are an exception, as they freeze exceptionally well.
7. Freeze unfrosted cookies for 9-12 months. Separate layers with waxed paper or plastic wrap.
8. Freeze frosted cookies uncovered until firm. Then pack them in an airtight container lined with plastic wrap or foil. They can be frozen for 2-3 months.
9. Cardboard containers are not recommended for storage, as the flavor and aroma from the box may be absorbed by the cookies.
10. Frozen cookies thaw in about 10 minutes at room temperature.
